content.text: Do 7 out of the following questions.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: State the Burnside’s theorem for solvability of a finite group.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Let \(H\) be a normal subgroup of a group \(G\). Prove that \(C_G(H)\) is a normal subgroup of \(G\).

subparts.label: (d)
subparts.text: Let \(G\) be a finite group of order 12. Prove that \(G\) has a non-trivial normal 2-subgroup (i.e., there is a subgroup \(T\) of \(G\) such that \(1\ne T\), and \(T\) is a 2-subgroup, and \(T\) is normal in \(G\)).

problem.text: Two permutation representations \(\phi_i:G\to\Sigma(X_i)\), (the symmetric group on \(X_i\)), are equivalent if there exists \(\alpha:X_1\to X_2\) such that for all \(g\in G\), \(x\in X_1\), we have \(x(g\phi_1)\alpha=x(\alpha)(g\phi_2)\). Prove or disprove that the following two permutation representations of \(S_4\) of degree 6 are equivalent:

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: on the right coset space of \(\langle(12)(34),(13)(24)\rangle\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: on the right coset space of \(\langle(12),(34)\rangle\).

problem.text: Let \(F_n\) be the free group on \(n\) generators \(x_1,\ldots,x_n\). Let \(F_n'\) denote the commutator subgroup. Prove that \(F_n/F_n'\) is the free abelian group on \(\{x_1,\ldots,x_n\}\).

In each of the following groups decide whether it is a semi-direct product of two of its proper subgroups: \(D_4\) (the dihedral group of order 8); \(Q_8\) (the quaternion group of order 8). Justify your answer.

Let \(A\) be a commutative ring with identity. Suppose that for each prime ideal \(P\), the local ring \(A_P\) has no nilpotent element \(\ne 0\). Show that \(A\) has no nilpotent element \(\ne 0\). If each \(A_P\) is an integral domain, is \(A\) necessarily an integral domain? Justify your answer.
Let \(A\) be a commutative ring with identity, \(a\) an ideal, \(M\) an \(A\)-module.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that \((A/a)\otimes_A M\) is isomorphic to \(M/aM\).

problem.text: Let \(A\subseteq B\) be integral domains, \(B\) integral over \(A\). Prove that \(B\) is a field if and only if \(A\) is a field.
Find a minimal primary decomposition for the following ideals. Also give the prime ideals to which the primary ideals belong.

subparts.label: (e)
subparts.text: Wedderburn’s theorem for finite dimensional simple algebras (or Wedderburn–Artin’s theorem for simple Artinian rings).
